title: Project Overview
description: Start with README.md to understand the project's purpose and vision. This React + Vite template is designed to provide a minimal but complete setup for React development with Hot Module Replacement (HMR) and ESLint rules preconfigured. The document outlines the two official Vite React plugins and guidance for expanding the linting configuration for production applications.

title: HTML Bootstrap
description: The index.html file is the entry point for the browser. It defines the root DOM container where React will mount the application and loads the main JavaScript bundle. Understanding this file reveals how Vite bridges the HTML entry point to the JavaScript application startup.

title: React Application Entry
description: The main.jsx file is the JavaScript entry point that Vite loads. It imports React, renders the App component into the DOM root, and imports the global stylesheet. This concise file demonstrates the React bootstrap pattern and shows how styles and components are composed together.

title: Main App Component
description: App.jsx is the root React component implementing the interactive counter demo and community links UI. It imports and displays logos, manages component state, and orchestrates the visual layout. This component demonstrates core React patterns: state management with useState, JSX rendering, and asset imports.
languageLesson: React components in JSX use curly braces {} to embed JavaScript expressions within markup. The useState hook manages component state, while event handlers like onClick connect user interactions to state updates. Assets (SVGs, images) are imported as module dependencies, enabling Vite to optimize and version them.

title: Styling Strategy
description: Two CSS files establish the project's styling approach: index.css defines global design tokens and resets applied site-wide, while App.css contains scoped styles for the App component. Together they demonstrate CSS organization in modern React projects, separating global concerns from component-specific styling. The 158-line App.css handles layout, animations, and component theming.

title: Build & Development Tools
description: The package.json, vite.config.js, and eslint.config.js files configure the entire development and build pipeline. package.json declares dependencies including React, Vite, and ESLint. vite.config.js enables the React plugin for JSX transformation and HMR. eslint.config.js enforces code quality rules across the JavaScript and JSX codebase. These three files are the foundation of the development experience and build output.
languageLesson: Vite uses a configuration file to enable plugins and optimize the build. The React plugin transpiles JSX and enables HMR so changes appear instantly in the browser without a full refresh. ESLint config files define which linting rules apply—in this project, basic JavaScript/JSX rules are enabled with room for TypeScript-aware rules in production applications.
